name: Repomix (Pack Codebase into AI-Friendly Format)

auth.notes: Local CLI tool; no auth needed (remote repos via GitHub URL)

performance.notes: Local file processing; speed depends on repo size

agent_readiness.notes: Packs entire codebase into a single file for LLM context. npx repomix or repomix --remote github.com/user/repo. Output formats: XML (Claude-optimized), Markdown, plain text. respects .gitignore. repomix.config.json for includes/excludes, custom instructions. Token counting. Security check (removes secrets). MCP server mode. Web UI at repomix.com. Successor to Repopack. Used for giving AI tools full codebase context for analysis, code review, Q&A.

gotchas: Large repos may exceed LLM context limits
Sensitive data may be included (use security check + .repomixignore)
XML output can be verbose for simple repos
No streaming — must process entire repo before output
best_when: Giving Claude/GPT-4 full codebase context for code review
Sharing codebase with AI for architecture questions
Creating context-rich prompts for AI pair programming
avoid_when: Very large repos that exceed LLM context windows
summary: Repomix packs your entire codebase into a single LLM-friendly file. npx repomix or --remote github.com/user/repo. XML/Markdown/plain text output. .gitignore-aware, secret detection, token counting. MCP server mode. The standard tool for giving AI tools full codebase context. Web UI at repomix.com. Use .repomixignore for sensitive files.
